formula: Quadratic Formula: x = (-b ± √(b²-4ac)) / 2a — solves ax²+bx+c = 0. Discriminant D = b²-4ac determines nature of roots: D>0 two distinct real, D=0 equal, D<0 complex.
Factoring: ax²+bx+c = a(x-r₁)(x-r₂) where r₁,r₂ are roots
Completing the square: x²+bx = (x+b/2)²-(b/2)²
Vieta's formulas: for ax²+bx+c=0, sum of roots = -b/a, product of roots = c/a. For cubic ax³+bx²+cx+d=0: Σr = -b/a, Σr₁r₂ = c/a, r₁r₂r₃ = -d/a
example: x²-7x+12=0 → sum=7, product=12 → roots are 3 and 4

formula: Arithmetic Progression (AP): nth term aₙ = a + (n-1)d. Sum of n terms Sₙ = n/2 · [2a + (n-1)d] = n/2 · (first + last)
Geometric Progression (GP): nth term aₙ = a·rⁿ⁻¹. Sum of n terms Sₙ = a(rⁿ-1)/(r-1) for r≠1. Sum of infinite GP (|r|<1): S∞ = a/(1-r)
Harmonic Progression (HP): a,b,c in HP iff 1/a,1/b,1/c in AP. Harmonic mean of a,b = 2ab/(a+b)
AM-GM-HM Inequality: For positive numbers, AM ≥ GM ≥ HM. AM = (a+b)/2, GM = √(ab), HM = 2ab/(a+b). Equality iff a=b.
Binomial Theorem: (a+b)ⁿ = Σ(k=0 to n) C(n,k)·aⁿ⁻ᵏ·bᵏ. General term Tₖ₊₁ = C(n,k)·aⁿ⁻ᵏ·bᵏ. Middle term when n is even: T_{n/2+1}
Binomial coefficient properties: C(n,r) = C(n,n-r). C(n,0)+C(n,1)+…+C(n,n) = 2ⁿ. C(n,0)-C(n,1)+C(n,2)-… = 0
Remainder Theorem: When polynomial p(x) is divided by (x-a), remainder = p(a). Factor Theorem: (x-a) is a factor of p(x) iff p(a) = 0
Partial Fractions: A/(x-a) + B/(x-b) for distinct linear factors. A/(x-a) + B/(x-a)² for repeated. (Ax+B)/(x²+px+q) for irreducible quadratic
Logarithm properties: log(ab) = log(a)+log(b), log(a/b) = log(a)-log(b), log(aⁿ) = n·log(a), logₐ(b) = ln(b)/ln(a), logₐ(b)·logᵦ(a) = 1
Sum of powers: Σk = n(n+1)/2, Σk² = n(n+1)(2n+1)/6, Σk³ = [n(n+1)/2]²
Complex Numbers: z = a+bi, |z| = √(a²+b²), arg(z) = arctan(b/a). Euler's form: z = |z|·e^(iθ) = |z|(cosθ + i·sinθ)
De Moivre's Theorem: (cosθ + i·sinθ)ⁿ = cos(nθ) + i·sin(nθ). nth roots of unity: e^(2πik/n) for k=0,1,…,n-1
Mathematical Induction: Step 1 – Base case (verify for n=1). Step 2 – Inductive step: Assume true for n=k, prove for n=k+1.
example: Prove Σ(1 to n) = n(n+1)/2: Base n=1: 1=1✓. If true for k, then Σ(1 to k+1) = k(k+1)/2 + (k+1) = (k+1)(k+2)/2 ✓

formula: Power Rule (derivative): d/dx [xⁿ] = n·xⁿ⁻¹
Chain Rule: d/dx [f(g(x))] = f'(g(x))·g'(x)
Product Rule: d/dx [f·g] = f'·g + f·g'
Quotient Rule: d/dx [f/g] = (f'·g - f·g') / g²
Integration by parts: ∫u dv = uv - ∫v du. LIATE rule for choosing u: Logarithmic → Inverse trig → Algebraic → Trig → Exponential
example: ∫x·eˣ dx: u=x, dv=eˣdx → x·eˣ - ∫eˣ dx = x·eˣ - eˣ + C

formula: Integration by substitution: ∫f(g(x))·g'(x)dx = ∫f(u)du where u=g(x)
Fundamental Theorem of Calculus: ∫ₐᵇ f(x)dx = F(b)-F(a) where F'=f
L'Hôpital's Rule: if lim f(x)/g(x) is 0/0 or ∞/∞, then lim f(x)/g(x) = lim f'(x)/g'(x)
Standard limits: lim(x→0) sin(x)/x = 1, lim(x→0) (1-cos(x))/x² = 1/2, lim(x→0) tan(x)/x = 1, lim(x→0) (eˣ-1)/x = 1, lim(x→0) ln(1+x)/x = 1, lim(x→∞)(1+1/x)ˣ = e
Sandwich/Squeeze Theorem: If g(x) ≤ f(x) ≤ h(x) near a, and lim g(x) = lim h(x) = L, then lim f(x) = L
Continuity: f is continuous at x=a iff lim(x→a) f(x) = f(a). Three conditions: f(a) exists, limit exists, limit equals f(a).
example: f(x)={x² for x≤1, 2x-1 for x>1} → f(1)=1, lim(x→1⁺)=1, lim(x→1⁻)=1 → continuous at x=1

formula: Rolle's Theorem: If f is continuous on [a,b], differentiable on (a,b), and f(a)=f(b), then ∃c∈(a,b) such that f'(c)=0
Mean Value Theorem (Lagrange): If f is continuous on [a,b] and differentiable on (a,b), then ∃c∈(a,b): f'(c) = (f(b)-f(a))/(b-a)
Second Derivative Test: If f'(c)=0, then f''(c)>0 → local minimum, f''(c)<0 → local maximum, f''(c)=0 → inconclusive (use first derivative test)
Implicit Differentiation: Differentiate both sides w.r.t. x, treat y as function of x, solve for dy/dx
Taylor Series: f(x) = Σ(n=0 to ∞) f⁽ⁿ⁾(a)/n! · (x-a)ⁿ. Maclaurin (a=0): eˣ=Σxⁿ/n!, sin(x)=Σ(-1)ⁿx²ⁿ⁺¹/(2n+1)!, cos(x)=Σ(-1)ⁿx²ⁿ/(2n)!
Definite Integral Properties: ∫ₐᵇ f(x)dx = -∫ᵇₐ f(x)dx. ∫₀ᵃ f(x)dx = ∫₀ᵃ f(a-x)dx (King property). If f(-x)=f(x) (even): ∫₋ₐᵃ f=2∫₀ᵃ f. If f(-x)=-f(x) (odd): ∫₋ₐᵃ f=0
example: ∫₀^π sin(x)dx using King: = ∫₀^π sin(π-x)dx = ∫₀^π sin(x)dx ✓. ∫₋₁¹ x³dx = 0 (odd function)

formula: Area between curves: A = ∫ₐᵇ |f(x)-g(x)| dx, where a,b are intersection points
example: Area between y=x² and y=x: intersection at x=0,1 → A = ∫₀¹(x-x²)dx = [x²/2-x³/3]₀¹ = 1/6

formula: Variable Separable DE: dy/dx = f(x)·g(y) → ∫dy/g(y) = ∫f(x)dx + C
example: dy/dx = xy → ∫dy/y = ∫xdx → ln|y| = x²/2+C → y = Ae^(x²/2)

formula: Linear First-Order DE: dy/dx + P(x)y = Q(x). Integrating factor μ = e^(∫P dx). Solution: y·μ = ∫Q·μ dx + C
example: dy/dx + y/x = x: IF=e^(∫1/x dx)=x → xy=∫x²dx=x³/3+C → y=x²/3+C/x

formula: Reduction formulas: ∫sinⁿ(x)dx = -sinⁿ⁻¹(x)cos(x)/n + (n-1)/n · ∫sinⁿ⁻²(x)dx. Wallis' formula for ∫₀^(π/2) sinⁿ(x)dx
Distance between two points: d = √((x₂-x₁)² + (y₂-y₁)²). Midpoint: ((x₁+x₂)/2, (y₁+y₂)/2)
Section formula: Point dividing (x₁,y₁) and (x₂,y₂) in ratio m:n = ((mx₂+nx₁)/(m+n), (my₂+ny₁)/(m+n)). External division: change + to -
Straight Line forms: Slope-intercept y=mx+c. Point-slope y-y₁=m(x-x₁). Two-point: (y-y₁)/(y₂-y₁) = (x-x₁)/(x₂-x₁). Intercept: x/a+y/b=1. General: ax+by+c=0
example: Line through (1,2) with slope 3: y-2=3(x-1) → y=3x-1

formula: Distance of point (x₁,y₁) from line ax+by+c=0: d = |ax₁+by₁+c| / √(a²+b²). Angle between two lines: tanθ = |m₁-m₂|/(1+m₁m₂)
Circle: (x-h)²+(y-k)²=r² (centre (h,k), radius r). General form: x²+y²+2gx+2fy+c=0, centre=(-g,-f), r=√(g²+f²-c)
Parabola standard forms: y²=4ax (opens right, focus (a,0), directrix x=-a). x²=4ay (opens up, focus (0,a), directrix y=-a). Latus rectum = 4a
example: y²=12x → 4a=12, a=3, focus=(3,0), directrix x=-3, LR=12

formula: Ellipse: x²/a²+y²/b²=1 (a>b). Eccentricity e=√(1-b²/a²). Foci (±ae,0). Sum of focal distances = 2a. Latus rectum = 2b²/a
Hyperbola: x²/a²-y²/b²=1. Eccentricity e=√(1+b²/a²)>1. Foci (±ae,0). Asymptotes y=±(b/a)x. |difference of focal distances| = 2a
Tangent to conic: For circle x²+y²=r²: xx₁+yy₁=r² at (x₁,y₁). For parabola y²=4ax: yy₁=2a(x+x₁). Condition for y=mx+c tangent to y²=4ax: c=a/m
Pythagorean Theorem: a² + b² = c² (right triangle)
Area of a circle: A = πr². Circumference = 2πr
Area of triangle: A = ½·b·h. Using coordinates: A = ½|x₁(y₂-y₃)+x₂(y₃-y₁)+x₃(y₁-y₂)|
Heron's formula: A = √[s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)] where s=(a+b+c)/2 is semi-perimeter
Pythagorean identities: sin²θ+cos²θ=1, 1+tan²θ=sec²θ, 1+cot²θ=csc²θ
Sum/Difference formulas: sin(A±B)=sinA·cosB±cosA·sinB. cos(A±B)=cosA·cosB∓sinA·sinB. tan(A±B)=(tanA±tanB)/(1∓tanA·tanB)
Double angle: sin(2A)=2sinA·cosA. cos(2A)=cos²A-sin²A=2cos²A-1=1-2sin²A. tan(2A)=2tanA/(1-tan²A)
Half angle: sin²(A/2)=(1-cosA)/2. cos²(A/2)=(1+cosA)/2. tan(A/2)=sinA/(1+cosA)=(1-cosA)/sinA
Product-to-Sum: 2sinA·cosB = sin(A+B)+sin(A-B). 2cosA·cosB = cos(A-B)+cos(A+B). Sum-to-Product: sinC+sinD = 2sin((C+D)/2)·cos((C-D)/2)
Law of cosines: c² = a²+b²-2ab·cos(C). Law of sines: a/sinA = b/sinB = c/sinC = 2R (circumradius)
Inverse trig ranges: sin⁻¹(x)∈[-π/2,π/2], cos⁻¹(x)∈[0,π], tan⁻¹(x)∈(-π/2,π/2). sin⁻¹(x)+cos⁻¹(x)=π/2. tan⁻¹(x)+tan⁻¹(1/x)=π/2 for x>0
example: sin⁻¹(1/2) = π/6 = 30°. tan⁻¹(1)+tan⁻¹(1) ≠ π/2 but tan⁻¹(1)+tan⁻¹(1)=π/2... wait, tan⁻¹(2)+tan⁻¹(1/2)=π/2 ✓

formula: General solutions: sinx=sinα → x=nπ+(-1)ⁿα. cosx=cosα → x=2nπ±α. tanx=tanα → x=nπ+α (n∈Z)
Determinant 2×2: det[[a,b],[c,d]] = ad-bc. Determinant 3×3 (expansion along first row): det = a₁₁(a₂₂a₃₃-a₂₃a₃₂) - a₁₂(a₂₁a₃₃-a₂₃a₃₁) + a₁₃(a₂₁a₃₂-a₂₂a₃₁)
Matrix multiplication (2×2): [[a,b],[c,d]]·[[e,f],[g,h]] = [[ae+bg,af+bh],[ce+dg,cf+dh]]. AB ≠ BA in general.
Inverse of 2×2 matrix: A⁻¹ = (1/det(A))·[[d,-b],[-c,a]] for A=[[a,b],[c,d]]. A matrix is invertible iff det(A)≠0
Cramer's Rule: For AX=B with det(A)≠0: xᵢ = det(Aᵢ)/det(A), where Aᵢ is A with column i replaced by B
Eigenvalue equation: det(A-λI)=0 gives eigenvalues. Eigenvector: (A-λI)v=0. Trace = sum of eigenvalues, det = product of eigenvalues
Cayley-Hamilton Theorem: Every square matrix satisfies its own characteristic equation. If p(λ)=0 is characteristic equation, then p(A)=O (zero matrix)
Properties of determinants: Swapping rows changes sign. Multiplying a row by k multiplies det by k. Adding multiple of one row to another doesn't change det. det(AB)=det(A)·det(B)
Dot product: a⃗·b⃗ = |a||b|cosθ = a₁b₁+a₂b₂+a₃b₃. Vectors perpendicular iff a⃗·b⃗=0. Projection of a⃗ on b⃗ = (a⃗·b⃗)/|b⃗|
Cross product: a⃗×b⃗ = |i  j  k; a₁ a₂ a₃; b₁ b₂ b₃|. |a⃗×b⃗| = |a||b|sinθ. Result is perpendicular to both. a⃗×b⃗ = -b⃗×a⃗
Scalar triple product: [a⃗ b⃗ c⃗] = a⃗·(b⃗×c⃗) = det[a₁ a₂ a₃; b₁ b₂ b₃; c₁ c₂ c₃]. Volume of parallelepiped = |[a⃗ b⃗ c⃗]|. Coplanar iff [a⃗ b⃗ c⃗]=0
Equation of line in 3D: r⃗ = a⃗ + λb⃗ (vector form). (x-x₁)/l = (y-y₁)/m = (z-z₁)/n (Cartesian form, direction ratios l,m,n)
Equation of plane: r⃗·n⃗ = d (vector form). ax+by+cz=d (Cartesian). Distance from point (x₀,y₀,z₀) to plane ax+by+cz+d=0: |ax₀+by₀+cz₀+d|/√(a²+b²+c²)
Angle between planes: cosθ = |n⃗₁·n⃗₂|/(|n⃗₁|·|n⃗₂|). Angle between line (direction b⃗) and plane (normal n⃗): sinφ = |b⃗·n⃗|/(|b⃗|·|n⃗|)
Bayes' Theorem: P(A|B) = P(B|A)·P(A)/P(B). Total Probability: P(B) = Σ P(B|Aᵢ)·P(Aᵢ)
Combinations: C(n,k) = n!/(k!·(n-k)!). Permutations: P(n,k) = n!/(n-k)!. C(n,k) = C(n,n-k)
example: C(10,3) = 120. P(10,3) = 720. Ways to choose 3 from 10 without order = 120

formula: Permutations with repetition: n!/(p!·q!·r!…) where p,q,r are frequencies of repeated elements
Conditional probability: P(A|B) = P(A∩B)/P(B). Independent events: P(A∩B)=P(A)·P(B). Mutually exclusive: P(A∪B)=P(A)+P(B)
Binomial Distribution: P(X=k) = C(n,k)·pᵏ·(1-p)ⁿ⁻ᵏ. Mean=np, Variance=np(1-p)
Mean & Variance: Mean μ = Σxᵢ/n. Variance σ² = Σ(xᵢ-μ)²/n = (Σxᵢ²/n) - μ². Standard deviation σ = √(variance)
Percentage: part/whole × 100. %change = (new-old)/old × 100. Successive %: after a% then b% → net = a+b+ab/100
Simple Interest: I=Prt. Compound Interest: A=P(1+r/n)^(nt). Amount after t years at rate r compounded n times per year
MISTAKE: √(a²+b²) ≠ a+b. The square root of a sum is NOT the sum of square roots.
MISTAKE: (a+b)² ≠ a²+b². Must expand: (a+b)² = a²+2ab+b².
MISTAKE: When dividing both sides of an inequality by a negative number, FLIP the inequality sign.
MISTAKE: d/dx[f·g] ≠ f'·g'. Must use product rule: f'g + fg'.
MISTAKE: log(a+b) ≠ log(a)+log(b). The log of a sum is NOT the sum of logs. log(a·b) = log(a)+log(b).
MISTAKE: Forgetting +C in indefinite integrals. ∫f(x)dx = F(x) + C, not just F(x).
example: ∫2x dx = x² + C, NOT just x²

formula: MISTAKE: Confusing P(n,r) and C(n,r). Permutation (order matters) vs Combination (order doesn't). P(n,r) = r! · C(n,r)
MISTAKE: sin⁻¹(sin(x)) ≠ x always. Only true when x ∈ [-π/2, π/2] for sin⁻¹. Check the principal value range.
MISTAKE: det(kA) ≠ k·det(A) for n×n matrix. Correct: det(kA) = kⁿ·det(A).
TEMPLATE – Optimization (Calculus): 1) Define objective f(x), 2) Find f'(x)=0, 3) Solve for critical points, 4) Use second derivative test, 5) Check domain boundaries.
TEMPLATE – System of Linear Equations: 1) Write in matrix form Ax=b, 2) Use elimination or Cramer's rule, 3) Check solution by substitution.
TEMPLATE – Probability word problems: 1) Identify sample space, 2) Define events, 3) Check independence/mutual exclusivity, 4) Apply formula (addition/multiplication rule, Bayes, combinations).
TEMPLATE – Conic Section Identification: 1) Write general form ax²+2hxy+by²+2gx+2fy+c=0, 2) Compute Δ=abc+2fgh-af²-bg²-ch². 3) If Δ≠0: h²<ab→ellipse, h²=ab→parabola, h²>ab→hyperbola.

TEMPLATE – Differential Equation: 1) Identify type (separable / linear / homogeneous / exact), 2) Apply appropriate method, 3) Find general solution with constant C, 4) Apply initial conditions if given.
example: dy/dx + 2y = e⁻ˣ → IF=e²ˣ → ye²ˣ = ∫eˣdx = eˣ+C → y = e⁻ˣ+Ce⁻²ˣ

formula: TEMPLATE – Vector/3D problems: 1) Express given info as position vectors or direction vectors, 2) Use dot/cross product as needed, 3) Find equation of line/plane, 4) Compute distances/angles.
example: Shortest distance between parallel lines r⃗=a⃗₁+λb⃗ and r⃗=a⃗₂+μb⃗: d = |(a⃗₂-a⃗₁)×b⃗|/|b⃗|
